Circular No. 01/2020/TT-BCT regulates the import of raw tobacco under the tariff rate quota for 2020.

regulates the import of raw tobacco under the tariff rate quota for 2020.
TRẦN TUẤN ANH
tax
LEGAL DOCUMENT

MINISTRY OF INDUSTRY AND TRADE

MINISTRY OF INDUSTRY AND TRADE

SOCIALIST REPUBLIC OF VIETNAM

Independence - Freedom - Happiness

No: 01/2020/TT-BCT

Hanoi, January 14, 2020

CIRCULAR

Regulating the import of raw tobacco

under the tariff rate quota for 2020

Pursuant to Decree No. 98/2017/NĐ-CP dated August 18, 2017 of the Government defining the functions, tasks, powers and organizational structure of the Ministry of Industry and Trade;

Pursuant to Decree No. 69/2018/NĐ-CP dated May 15, 2018 of the Government detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management;

At the proposal of the Director General of the Import and Export Department,

The Minister of Industry and Trade promulgates the Circular regulating the import of raw tobacco under the tariff rate quota for 2020.

Article 1. Quantity of tariff rate quota for 2020

The quantity of raw tobacco (HS code 2401) imported under the tariff rate quota for 2020 is 59,098 tons.

Article 2. Subjects and methods of allocation of import tariff rate quota

The allocation of import tariff rate quota for raw tobacco is carried out in accordance with the provisions of Decree No. 69/2018/NĐ-CP dated May 15, 2018 of the Government detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management and Circular No. 12/2018/TT-BCT dated June 15, 2018 of the Ministry of Industry and Trade detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management.

Article 3. Effectiveness

This Circular takes effect from February 29, 2020 to December 31, 2020./.

MINISTER

Trần Tuấn Anh

paragraphs: Regulations on the importation of raw tobacco materials
Pursuant to Decree No. 98/2017/ND-CP dated August 18, 2017 of the Government defining the functions, tasks, powers, and organizational structure of the Ministry of Industry and Trade;
Pursuant to Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018 of the Government detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management;
At the proposal of the Director of the Import-Export Department,
The Minister of Industry and Trade issues the Circular regulating the importation of raw tobacco materials under the tariff quota in 2020.
Article 1. Import tariff quota quantity in 2020
The quantity of raw tobacco materials (HS code 2401) imported under the tariff quota in 2020 is 59,098 tons.
Article 2. Subjects and methods for allocating import tariff quotas

The allocation of import tariff quotas for raw tobacco materials is implemented according to the provisions of Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018 of the Government detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management and Circular No. 12/2018/TT-BCT dated June 15, 2018 of the Ministry of Industry and Trade detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management.
This Circular is effective from February 29, 2020, to December 31, 2020./.
content.text:
LEGAL DOCUMENT

MINISTRY OF INDUSTRY AND TRADE

MINISTRY OF INDUSTRY AND TRADE

SOCIALIST REPUBLIC OF VIETNAM

Independence - Freedom - Happiness

No.: 01/2020/TT-BCT

Hanoi, January 14, 2020

CIRCULAR

Regulation on the import of raw tobacco

according to the tariff quota in 2020

Pursuant to Decree No. 98/2017/ND-CP dated August 18, 2017, of the Government on the functions, tasks, powers, and organizational structure of the Ministry of Industry and Trade;

Pursuant to Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018, of the Government detailing some articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management;

Upon the proposal of the Director of the Import-Export Department,

the Minister of Industry and Trade issues the Circular regulating the import of raw tobacco according to the tariff quota in 2020.

Article 1. Import tariff quota volume in 2020

The volume of raw tobacco (HS code 2401) imported according to the tariff quota in 2020 is 59,098 tons.

Article 2. Subjects and method of tariff quota allocation for import

The allocation of tariff quotas for the import of raw tobacco is implemented according to the regulations in Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018, of the Government detailing some articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management and Circular No. 12/2018/TT-BCT dated June 15, 2018, of the Ministry of Industry and Trade detailing some articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management.

Article 3. Effective Date

This Circular is effective from February 29, 2020, to December 31, 2020./.

MINISTER

Tran Tuan Anh
